text: January 1: The dwarf planet Ceres was discovered by Giuseppe Piazzi, 1801.
January 2: Luna 1 launched, becoming the first spacecraft to leave Earth orbit, 1959.
January 3: China's Chang'e 4 became the first spacecraft to land on the Moon's far side, 2019.
January 4: NASA's Spirit rover landed in Gusev Crater on Mars, 2004.
January 5: Astronomers imaged the dwarf planet Eris, then thought larger than Pluto, 2005.
January 6: NASA's Lunar Prospector launched to map the Moon's surface and resources, 1998.
January 7: Galileo Galilei first observed the moons of Jupiter through his telescope, 1610.
January 8: Galileo Galilei, who discovered Jupiter's four largest moons, died, 1642.
January 9: Astronomers announced the first confirmed exoplanets, orbiting a pulsar, 1992.
January 10: The US Army's Project Diana bounced radar signals off the Moon, 1946.
January 11: William Herschel discovered Uranus's moons Titania and Oberon, 1787.
January 12: NASA's Deep Impact spacecraft launched toward comet Tempel 1, 2005.
January 13: Rakesh Sharma, who became the first Indian citizen in space, was born, 1949.
January 14: ESA's Huygens probe landed on Saturn's moon Titan, 2005.
January 15: Stardust's sample capsule returned to Earth carrying comet dust, 2006.
January 16: Soyuz 4 and Soyuz 5 completed the first docking of two crewed spacecraft, 1969.
January 17: Clyde Tombaugh, who discovered Pluto in 1930, died, 1997.
January 18: The Tagish Lake meteorite fell over Yukon, Canada, yielding pristine samples, 2000.
January 19: NASA's New Horizons spacecraft launched on its journey to Pluto, 2006.
January 20: Buzz Aldrin, the second person to walk on the Moon, was born, 1930.
January 21: John Couch Adams, who predicted Neptune's existence through mathematics, died, 1892.
January 22: Apollo 5 launched on an uncrewed test flight of the lunar module, 1968.
January 23: NASA received its last signal from the Pioneer 10 spacecraft, 2003.
January 24: Voyager 2 made its closest approach to Uranus, its only visitor to date, 1986.
January 25: NASA's Opportunity rover landed on Mars at Meridiani Planum, 2004.
January 26: The 200-inch Hale Telescope at Palomar Observatory achieved first light, 1949.
January 27: A launch pad fire killed the Apollo 1 crew during a training test, 1967.
January 28: The Space Shuttle Challenger broke apart 73 seconds after launch, 1986.
January 29: The Soviet Phobos 2 probe entered orbit around Mars, 1989.
January 30: Amateur astronomer Yuji Hyakutake discovered a comet that became a great comet, 1996.
January 31: Explorer 1, the first US satellite, launched into orbit, 1958.
February 1: A large meteoroid exploded over the Pacific near the Marshall Islands, 1994.
February 2: NASA's Ranger 6 probe struck the Moon, but its cameras had failed, 1964.
February 3: The Soviet Luna 9 made the first soft landing on the Moon, 1966.
February 4: Johannes Kepler met Tycho Brahe for the first time, in Prague, 1600.
February 5: Mariner 10 flew past Venus, performing the first interplanetary gravity assist, 1974.
February 6: Alan Shepard hit golf balls on the Moon during Apollo 14, 1971.
February 7: Bruce McCandless made the first untethered spacewalk using a jetpack, 1984.
February 8: The Allende meteorite fell over Mexico, becoming one of the most studied, 1969.
February 9: Space Shuttle Discovery docked with the ISS, delivering the Destiny module, 2001.
February 10: Scientists achieved the first radar contact with Venus, 1958.
February 11: Japan launched Ohsumi, becoming the fourth nation to orbit a satellite, 1970.
February 12: NEAR Shoemaker became the first spacecraft to land on an asteroid, Eros, 2001.
February 13: Space Shuttle Discovery retrieved Hubble for its second servicing mission, 1997.
February 14: Voyager 1 captured the Pale Blue Dot photograph of Earth from deep space, 1990.
February 15: A meteor exploded over Chelyabinsk, Russia, injuring over a thousand people, 2013.
February 16: Gerard Kuiper discovered Miranda, a moon of Uranus, 1948.
February 17: Vanguard 2, the first satellite designed to observe cloud cover, launched, 1959.
February 18: Clyde Tombaugh identified Pluto on photographic plates at Lowell Observatory, 1930.
February 19: Nicolaus Copernicus, who proposed a Sun-centered solar system, was born, 1473.
February 20: John Glenn became the first American to orbit Earth, aboard Friendship 7, 1962.
February 21: The Soviet Luna 20 soft-landed on the Moon to drill a highland sample, 1972.
February 22: The Soviet Cosmos 110 carried two dogs on a record 22-day spaceflight, 1966.
February 23: Light from Supernova 1987A, the closest supernova in centuries, reached Earth, 1987.
February 24: Jocelyn Bell Burnell's team announced the discovery of the first pulsar, 1968.
February 25: The Soviet Luna 20 returned to Earth carrying samples of lunar highlands, 1972.
February 26: A total solar eclipse crossed the northern United States and Canada, 1979.
February 27: Bernard Lyot, inventor of the coronagraph for studying the Sun, was born, 1897.
February 28: Discoverer 1, the first satellite launched into polar orbit, lifted off, 1959.
February 29: NASA astronaut Jack Lousma, who flew Skylab 3 and STS-3, was born, 1936.
March 1: The Soviet Venera 3 became the first spacecraft to reach another planet's surface, 1966.
March 2: Pioneer 10 launched, later becoming the first craft to leave the solar system, 1972.
March 3: Apollo 9 launched to test the lunar module in Earth orbit, 1969.
March 4: Pioneer 4 became the first US spacecraft to fly past the Moon, 1959.
March 5: Voyager 1 made its closest approach to Jupiter, 1979.
March 6: Valentina Tereshkova, who became the first woman in space, was born, 1937.
March 7: NASA's Kepler space telescope launched to search for exoplanets, 2009.
March 8: Voyager 1 images revealed the first active volcano beyond Earth, on Io, 1979.
March 9: Yuri Gagarin, who became the first human in space, was born, 1934.
March 10: Astronomers discovered rings around Uranus during a stellar occultation, 1977.
March 11: Urbain Le Verrier, who predicted Neptune's existence through mathematics, was born, 1811.
March 12: The Soviet Mars 6 probe returned the first atmospheric data from Mars, 1974.
March 13: William Herschel discovered the planet Uranus, 1781.
March 14: Johannes Winkler launched Europe's first successful liquid-fueled rocket, 1931.
March 15: Astronomers announced the discovery of Sedna, one of the most distant known Solar System objects, 2004.
March 16: Robert Goddard launched the world's first liquid-fueled rocket, 1926.
March 17: Vanguard 1, now the oldest satellite still in orbit, launched, 1958.
March 18: Alexei Leonov performed the first spacewalk in history, 1965.
March 19: Percival Lowell photographed Pluto, unaware of what he had captured, 1915.
March 20: The European Space Research Organisation, ESA's forerunner, began operations, 1964.
March 21: Antonia Maury, astronomer who devised a stellar classification system, was born, 1866.
March 22: Comet Hale-Bopp made its closest approach to Earth, 1997.
March 23: The Russian space station Mir reentered the atmosphere over the Pacific, 2001.
March 24: Walter Baade, astronomer known for defining stellar populations, was born, 1893.
March 25: Christiaan Huygens discovered Titan, Saturn's largest moon, 1655.
March 26: Explorer 3, the third successful US satellite, launched, 1958.
March 27: Yuri Gagarin, the first human in space, died in a plane crash, 1968.
March 28: Heinrich Olbers discovered Pallas, the second known asteroid, 1802.
March 29: Mariner 10 became the first spacecraft to fly past Mercury, 1974.
March 30: Joseph Walker flew the X-15 above 50 kilometers, the US definition of spaceflight, 1961.
March 31: Luna 10 launched from the USSR, days before becoming the first spacecraft to orbit the Moon, 1966.
April 1: TIROS-1, the first weather satellite, was launched by NASA, 1960.
April 2: The Soviet Union launched Zond 1 toward Venus, though contact was later lost, 1964.
April 3: Luna 10 became the first spacecraft to orbit the Moon, 1966.
April 4: Space Shuttle Challenger launched on STS-6, its maiden flight, 1983.
April 5: The Compton Gamma Ray Observatory launched aboard Atlantis on STS-37, 1991.
April 6: Intelsat 1 'Early Bird,' the first commercial communications satellite, launched, 1965.
April 7: NASA's Mars Odyssey orbiter launched toward the Red Planet, 2001.
April 8: Gemini 1 flew the first uncrewed test of the Gemini spacecraft, 1964.
April 9: NASA introduced the Mercury Seven, its first group of astronauts, 1959.
April 10: The Event Horizon Telescope released humanity's first image of a black hole, 2019.
April 11: Apollo 13 launched toward the Moon, 1970.
April 12: Yuri Gagarin became the first human in space aboard Vostok 1, 1961.
April 13: An oxygen tank explosion crippled Apollo 13 en route to the Moon, 1970.
April 14: Space Shuttle Columbia landed, completing the first shuttle mission, STS-1, 1981.
April 15: President Obama outlined a new NASA policy at Kennedy Space Center, 2010.
April 16: Apollo 16 launched on the fifth crewed Moon landing mission, 1972.
April 17: The Apollo 13 crew splashed down safely in the Pacific Ocean, 1970.
April 18: NASA's TESS spacecraft launched to search for planets beyond our solar system, 2018.
April 19: The Soviet Union launched Salyut 1, the world's first space station, 1971.
April 20: Apollo 16's lunar module landed in the Descartes highlands, 1972.
April 21: Perseverance's MOXIE device produced the first oxygen from Mars's atmosphere, 2021.
April 22: Chris Hadfield performed the first spacewalk by a Canadian, on STS-100, 2001.
April 23: Soyuz 1 launched Vladimir Komarov on the first crewed Soyuz flight, 1967.
April 24: The Hubble Space Telescope launched aboard Space Shuttle Discovery, 1990.
April 25: The Hubble Space Telescope was deployed from Discovery's cargo bay, 1990.
April 26: Ranger 4 struck the Moon's far side, the first US craft to reach another world, 1962.
April 27: Apollo 16 splashed down, ending the fifth crewed Moon landing, 1972.
April 28: Dennis Tito launched aboard Soyuz TM-32 as the world's first space tourist, 2001.
April 29: China launched the Tianhe core module, the start of its Tiangong space station, 2021.
April 30: The Italian-Dutch X-ray satellite BeppoSAX launched from Cape Canaveral, 1996.
May 1: Physicist James Van Allen announced the discovery of Earth's radiation belts, 1958.
May 2: Athanasius Kircher, who studied sunspots and has a Moon crater named for him, was born, 1601.
May 3: Edmond Halley accurately predicted the total solar eclipse over London, 1715.
May 4: The Magellan spacecraft launched toward Venus aboard Space Shuttle Atlantis, 1989.
May 5: Alan Shepard became the first American in space aboard Freedom 7, 1961.
May 6: Space Shuttle Discovery landed after an 8-day Department of Defense mission, 1991.
May 7: Space Shuttle Endeavour launched on its maiden flight, STS-49, 1992.
May 8: Mariner 8 fell into the Atlantic after a launch failure meant for Mars, 1971.
May 9: Japan's Hayabusa spacecraft launched on its asteroid sample-return mission, 2003.
May 10: Soviet Mars probe Kosmos 419 was stranded in Earth orbit after a timer error, 1971.
May 11: Space Shuttle Atlantis launched on STS-125, the final Hubble servicing mission, 2009.
May 12: RAND issued a report outlining the feasibility of an orbiting satellite, 1946.
May 13: A major solar storm disrupted telegraph networks worldwide, 1921.
May 14: Skylab, the first US space station, launched atop the last Saturn V rocket, 1973.
May 15: Gordon Cooper closed out Project Mercury aboard Faith 7 on Mercury-Atlas 9, 1963.
May 16: Nancy Grace Roman, later called the 'Mother of Hubble,' was born, 1925.
May 17: A previously unknown comet was spotted during a solar eclipse in Egypt, 1882.
May 18: Apollo 10 launched as a full dress rehearsal for the Moon landing, 1969.
May 19: The ESA-China Smile mission launched to study the solar wind's effect on Earth, 2026.
May 20: Pioneer Venus 1 launched, becoming the first US spacecraft to orbit Venus, 1978.
May 21: Astronomer Williamina Fleming, discoverer of the Horsehead Nebula, died, 1911.
May 22: Apollo 10's lunar module made its closest approach to the Moon's surface, 1969.
May 23: A powerful solar flare jammed US missile-warning radar systems, 1967.
May 24: Nicolaus Copernicus, author of the heliocentric model of the solar system, died, 1543.
May 25: President Kennedy pledged to land a man on the Moon within the decade, 1961.
May 26: Apollo 10 splashed down after its dress-rehearsal flight to the Moon, 1969.
May 27: Space Shuttle Discovery launched on STS-96, the first shuttle docking at the ISS, 1999.
May 28: The Soviet Union launched Mars 3, which achieved the first soft landing on Mars, 1971.
May 29: A solar eclipse allowed astronomers to confirm Einstein's general relativity, 1919.
May 30: SpaceX's Crew Dragon launched Behnken and Hurley, the first US commercial crewed flight, 2020.
May 31: The European Space Agency formally began operations, 1975.
June 1: Cosmonaut Georgy Dobrovolsky, later commander of the first space station crew, was born, 1928.
June 2: Surveyor 1 achieved the first US soft landing on the Moon, 1966.
June 3: Ed White made the first spacewalk by an American, during Gemini 4, 1965.
June 4: SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket completed its successful maiden flight, 2010.
June 5: Mathematician John Couch Adams, who predicted Neptune's existence, was born, 1819.
June 6: Boeing's Starliner docked with the ISS on its first crewed test flight, 2024.
June 7: The Extreme Ultraviolet Explorer satellite launched to survey the sky in UV light, 1992.
June 8: Astronomer Giovanni Cassini, discoverer of four moons of Saturn, was born, 1625.
June 9: The Soviet Vega 1 spacecraft released its probe toward the clouds of Venus, 1985.
June 10: NASA's Spirit rover launched toward Mars, 2003.
June 11: The Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope launched to study the high-energy universe, 2008.
June 12: The Soviet Union launched Venera 4 toward Venus, 1967.
June 13: Pioneer 10 crossed Neptune's orbit, becoming the first craft to leave the planets behind, 1983.
June 14: Albert II, a rhesus monkey, became the first mammal launched into space, 1949.
June 15: Europe's Ariane 4 rocket launched on its maiden flight, 1988.
June 16: Valentina Tereshkova became the first woman in space aboard Vostok 6, 1963.
June 17: STS-51-G carried Sultan bin Salman Al Saud, the first Arab astronaut, into orbit, 1985.
June 18: Sally Ride became the first American woman in space aboard STS-7, 1983.
June 19: NASA's Viking 1 spacecraft entered orbit around Mars, 1976.
June 20: A German V-2 test flight became the first human-made object to reach space, 1944.
June 21: SpaceShipOne completed the first privately funded crewed spaceflight, 2004.
June 22: Astronomer James Christy discovered Charon, Pluto's largest moon, 1978.
June 23: The Soviet Union launched the Meteor 1-05 weather satellite, 1970.
June 24: Kenneth Arnold's sighting of nine objects near Mount Rainier coined the term flying saucer, 1947.
June 25: China's Chang'e 6 probe returned the first samples from the Moon's far side, 2024.
June 26: Astronomer Charles Messier, cataloguer of nebulae and star clusters, was born, 1730.
June 27: Space Shuttle Atlantis launched on STS-71, the first shuttle docking with Mir, 1995.
June 28: SpaceX's Falcon 9 broke apart during the failed CRS-7 cargo launch, 2015.
June 29: Astronomer George Ellery Hale, builder of Mount Wilson and Palomar, was born, 1868.
June 30: A massive explosion flattened Siberian forest in the Tunguska meteor event, 1908.
July 1: Cassini spacecraft entered orbit around Saturn, becoming its first artificial satellite, 2004.
July 2: ESA's Giotto probe launched toward Halley's Comet aboard an Ariane 1 rocket, 1985.
July 3: Japan's Nozomi probe launched toward Mars, the nation's first Mars mission, 1998.
July 4: Mars Pathfinder landed on Mars, later releasing the Sojourner rover, 1997.
July 5: Isaac Newton's Principia Mathematica, laying out the laws of motion and gravity, was published, 1687.
July 6: Astronomer Seth Nicholson discovered Jupiter's moon Lysithea, 1938.
July 7: NASA's Opportunity rover launched toward Mars, 2003.
July 8: Space Shuttle Atlantis launched on STS-135, the final space shuttle mission, 2011.
July 9: Voyager 2 made its closest approach to Jupiter, 1979.
July 10: Telstar 1, the first active communications satellite, launched into orbit, 1962.
July 11: Skylab, America's first space station, fell to Earth over the Indian Ocean and Australia, 1979.
July 12: Space Shuttle Atlantis launched on STS-104, delivering the ISS Quest airlock, 2001.
July 13: The Soviet Union launched Luna 15, racing to beat Apollo 11 to lunar samples, 1969.
July 14: New Horizons made the first close flyby of Pluto, 2015.
July 15: Apollo and Soyuz spacecraft launched hours apart for the first joint US-Soviet flight, 1975.
July 16: The first fragment of Comet Shoemaker-Levy 9 struck Jupiter, 1994.
July 17: Apollo and Soyuz crews docked in orbit, sharing the first handshake in space, 1975.
July 18: India launched its SLV-3 rocket, placing the Rohini satellite in orbit, 1980.
July 19: Pilot Joe Walker flew the X-15 above the Karman line, reaching space, 1963.
July 20: Apollo 11 landed on the Moon, 1969.
July 21: Atlantis landed after STS-135, closing NASA's 30-year space shuttle program, 2011.
July 22: NASA's Mariner 1 probe was destroyed after veering off course moments after launch, 1962.
July 23: China's Tianwen-1 launched, sending an orbiter and rover to Mars on its first attempt, 2020.
July 24: Bumper 8 became the first rocket launched from Cape Canaveral, 1950.
July 25: Svetlana Savitskaya became the first woman to walk in space, 1984.
July 26: Space Shuttle Discovery launched on STS-114, returning shuttles to flight after Columbia, 2005.
July 27: The longest total lunar eclipse of the 21st century darkened the Moon, 2018.
July 28: Skylab 3, the second crewed Skylab mission, launched with a three-man crew, 1973.
July 29: President Eisenhower signed the act creating NASA, 1958.
July 30: Apollo 15 landed on the Moon near Hadley Rille, 1971.
July 31: Ranger 7 returned the first close-up photographs of the Moon before impact, 1964.
August 1: Lunar Orbiter 5 launched, completing NASA's survey of candidate Apollo landing sites, 1967.
August 2: Space Shuttle Atlantis launched on STS-43, deploying a NASA relay satellite, 1991.
August 3: MESSENGER launched on its long journey to orbit Mercury, 2004.
August 4: The Phoenix lander launched toward the northern plains of Mars, 2007.
August 5: Neil Armstrong, the first person to walk on the Moon, was born, 1930.
August 6: NASA's Curiosity rover landed in Gale Crater on Mars, 2012.
August 7: Apollo 15 splashed down in the Pacific, ending its record lunar mission, 1971.
August 8: The Genesis spacecraft launched to collect samples of the solar wind, 2001.
August 9: The Soviet Union launched Luna 24, its last robotic lunar sample-return mission, 1976.
August 10: NASA's Magellan spacecraft entered orbit around Venus to map its surface by radar, 1990.
August 11: Soviet cosmonaut Andrian Nikolayev launched aboard Vostok 3 on a 94-hour flight, 1962.
August 12: Echo 1, the first passive communications satellite, launched into orbit, 1960.
August 13: Comet hunter Carolyn Shoemaker, co-discoverer of Shoemaker-Levy 9, died, 2021.
August 14: Explorer 6 transmitted the first photograph of Earth taken from orbit, 1959.
August 15: Radio astronomers recorded the unexplained Wow signal from deep space, 1977.
August 16: Apollo 14 astronaut Stuart Roosa, who flew tree seeds to the Moon, was born, 1933.
August 17: The Soviet Union launched Venera 7, which later made the first landing on another planet, 1970.
August 18: The Discoverer 14 satellite launched, later yielding the first film recovered from orbit, 1960.
August 19: Sputnik 5 launched with dogs Belka and Strelka, the first animals to orbit and return alive, 1960.
August 20: Voyager 2 launched on its grand tour of the outer planets, 1977.
August 21: A total solar eclipse crossed the continental United States coast to coast, 2017.
August 22: Skylab 4 commander Gerald Carr, who logged a then-record 84 days in space, was born, 1932.
August 23: India's Chandrayaan-3 became the first spacecraft to land near the Moon's south pole, 2023.
August 24: The International Astronomical Union voted to reclassify Pluto as a dwarf planet, 2006.
August 25: Voyager 2 made its closest approach to Neptune, humanity's only visit to the planet, 1989.
August 26: Sigmund Jahn launched aboard Soyuz 31, becoming the first German in space, 1978.
August 27: Mariner 2 launched toward Venus, becoming the first successful planetary probe, 1962.
August 28: Galileo's flyby of asteroid Ida revealed its moon Dactyl, the first known asteroid moon, 1993.
August 29: Comet 2I/Borisov, only the second interstellar object found, was discovered, 2019.
August 30: Space Shuttle Discovery launched on STS-41-D, its maiden flight, 1984.
August 31: North Korea attempted to launch its first satellite, Kwangmyongsong-1, 1998.
September 1: Pioneer 11 became the first spacecraft to fly past Saturn, 1979.
September 2: Chandrayaan-2's Vikram lander separated from its orbiter ahead of a landing attempt, 2019.
September 3: Viking 2 landed on Mars in the Utopia Planitia region, 1976.
September 4: The International Astronautical Federation was founded in London, 1951.
September 5: Voyager 1 launched, later becoming the most distant human-made object, 1977.
September 6: Johannes Kepler made the first recorded telescopic observation of a comet, 1618.
September 7: Chandrayaan-2's Vikram lander lost contact during its descent to the Moon, 2019.
September 8: OSIRIS-REx launched to collect a sample from asteroid Bennu, 2016.
September 9: Space Shuttle Atlantis launched on STS-115, adding solar array trusses to the ISS, 2006.
September 10: Japan's H-IIB rocket debuted, launching the HTV-1 cargo ship toward the ISS, 2009.
September 11: The ICE spacecraft flew through Comet Giacobini-Zinner's tail, the first comet flyby, 1985.
September 12: President Kennedy gave his We Choose to Go to the Moon speech at Rice University, 1962.
September 13: The US Air Force destroyed the Solwind satellite in its only air-launched ASAT test, 1985.
September 14: Luna 2 struck the Moon, becoming the first spacecraft to reach another world, 1959.
September 15: Cassini plunged into Saturn's atmosphere, deliberately ending its 13-year mission, 2017.
September 16: Space Shuttle Atlantis docked with Mir on STS-79, exchanging long-duration crew, 1996.
September 17: The X-15 rocket plane made its first powered flight, 1959.
September 18: Voyager 1 took the first photograph showing Earth and the Moon together, 1977.
September 19: Israel launched Ofeq-1, its first satellite, 1988.
September 20: Surveyor 2 launched toward the Moon but later tumbled after a thruster failure, 1966.
September 21: NASA deliberately crashed Galileo into Jupiter to protect its moon Europa, 2003.
September 22: Surveyor 2 crashed on the Moon after its mid-course correction failed, 1966.
September 23: Johann Galle discovered Neptune within a degree of its predicted position, 1846.
September 24: India's Mars Orbiter Mission, Mangalyaan, entered orbit around Mars, 2014.
September 25: The Skylab 3 crew splashed down after a then-record 59 days in space, 1973.
September 26: NASA's DART spacecraft deliberately struck the asteroid Dimorphos, 2022.
September 27: NASA's Dawn spacecraft launched toward the asteroid belt to study Vesta and Ceres, 2007.
September 28: SpaceX's Falcon 1 became the first privately developed rocket to reach orbit, 2008.
September 29: Space Shuttle Discovery launched on STS-26, returning shuttles to flight after Challenger, 1988.
September 30: Space Shuttle Endeavour launched on STS-68, carrying the Space Radar Laboratory, 1994.
October 1: NASA began operations as the United States' civilian space agency, 1958.
October 2: NASA launched Explorer 14 to study Earth's radiation belts, 1962.
October 3: Germany's A-4 rocket, later called the V-2, became the first object to reach space, 1942.
October 4: The Soviet Union launched Sputnik 1, the first artificial satellite, 1957.
October 5: Shuttle Challenger launched on STS-41-G, with the first US spacewalk by a woman, 1984.
October 6: Astronomers announced 51 Pegasi b, the first exoplanet found orbiting a sun-like star, 1995.
October 7: Luna 3 transmitted the first photographs of the Moon's far side, 1959.
October 8: William Lassell discovered Triton, the largest moon of Neptune, 1846.
October 9: NASA's LCROSS probe deliberately impacted the Moon to search for water ice, 2009.
October 10: The Outer Space Treaty, the foundation of international space law, entered into force, 1967.
October 11: Apollo 7, the first crewed Apollo mission, launched into Earth orbit, 1968.
October 12: Voskhod 1 launched, carrying the first multi-person crew into space, 1964.
October 13: William Shatner flew aboard Blue Origin's New Shepard, becoming the oldest person in space, 2021.
October 14: NASA's Europa Clipper launched to study Jupiter's icy moon Europa, 2024.
October 15: Shenzhou 5 carried Yang Liwei, China's first astronaut, into orbit, 2003.
October 16: GOES-1, first in NASA's geostationary weather satellite series, launched, 1975.
October 17: Mae Jemison, the first African American woman in space, was born, 1956.
October 18: Venera 4 became the first probe to measure the atmosphere of another planet, Venus, 1967.
October 19: Astronauts Christina Koch and Jessica Meir conducted the first all-female spacewalk, 2019.
October 20: NASA's OSIRIS-REx spacecraft touched down on asteroid Bennu to collect a sample, 2020.
October 21: Soyuz TM-33 launched Claudie Haigneré, the first European woman to visit the ISS, 2001.
October 22: Venera 9 landed on Venus and sent the first images from the surface of another planet, 1975.
October 23: Shuttle Discovery launched on STS-120, delivering the Harmony module to the ISS, 2007.
October 24: China launched Chang'e 1, its first lunar orbiter, 2007.
October 25: Venera 10 landed on Venus and transmitted images for 65 minutes, 1975.
October 26: Sputnik 1 stopped transmitting after its batteries died, 22 days after launch, 1957.
October 27: The Saturn I rocket, precursor to the Saturn V, made its first successful flight, 1961.
October 28: The UK launched Prospero, becoming the sixth nation to orbit a satellite, 1971.
October 29: John Glenn returned to space aboard Shuttle Discovery at age 77, 1998.
October 30: The Soviet Union launched the Venera 13 probe toward Venus, 1981.
October 31: Soyuz TM-31 launched Expedition 1, starting continuous crewed presence on the ISS, 2000.
November 1: The Soviet Union launched Mars 1, the first probe aimed at the Red Planet, 1962.
November 2: Expedition 1 arrived at the ISS, beginning an unbroken run of human habitation, 2000.
November 3: The Soviet Union launched Sputnik 2, carrying the dog Laika into orbit, 1957.
November 4: The Soviet Union launched the Venera 14 probe toward Venus, 1981.
November 5: India launched Mangalyaan, its first interplanetary probe, toward Mars, 2013.
November 6: NASA launched Lunar Orbiter 2 to photograph future Apollo landing sites, 1966.
November 7: NASA launched the Mars Global Surveyor orbiter, 1996.
November 8: Edmond Halley, who predicted the return of the comet later named for him, was born, 1656.
November 9: Astronomer and science communicator Carl Sagan was born, 1934.
November 10: Luna 17 launched, carrying Lunokhod 1, the first robotic rover on another world, 1970.
November 11: Gemini 12, the last crewed Gemini mission, launched with Buzz Aldrin aboard, 1966.
November 12: Voyager 1 made its closest approach to Saturn, 1980.
November 13: NASA launched HEAO-2, later named the Einstein Observatory, the first imaging X-ray telescope, 1978.
November 14: Mariner 9 entered orbit around Mars, the first spacecraft to orbit another planet, 1971.
November 15: The Soviet shuttle Buran flew its only, uncrewed, orbital mission, 1988.
November 16: The Arecibo Observatory broadcast a radio message toward a distant star cluster, 1974.
November 17: ESA launched the Infrared Space Observatory to study the cosmos in infrared light, 1995.
November 18: NASA's MAVEN spacecraft launched to study the Martian atmosphere, 2013.
November 19: Apollo 12 astronauts Pete Conrad and Alan Bean landed on the Moon, 1969.
November 20: Zarya, the first module of the International Space Station, launched, 1998.
November 21: A Dnepr rocket launched a then-record 32 satellites on a single mission, 2013.
November 22: ESA's Swarm mission launched three satellites to study Earth's magnetic field, 2013.
November 23: The Soviet N1 moon rocket's fourth and final test flight ended in failure, 1972.
November 24: English astronomer Jeremiah Horrocks observed the first recorded transit of Venus, 1639.
November 25: Albert Einstein presented the field equations of general relativity, 1915.
November 26: NASA's Curiosity rover launched on its way to Mars, 2011.
November 27: Anders Celsius, inventor of the Celsius temperature scale, was born, 1701.
November 28: Mariner 4 launched and later made the first successful flyby of Mars, 1964.
November 29: Mercury-Atlas 5 carried the chimpanzee Enos on two orbits of Earth, 1961.
November 30: The Soviet Union launched the Zond 2 probe toward Mars, 1964.
December 1: China launched Chang'e 3, its first lunar lander, carrying the Yutu rover, 2013.
December 2: The Solar and Heliospheric Observatory, SOHO, launched to study the Sun, 1995.
December 3: Pioneer 10 made the first flyby of Jupiter, 1973.
December 4: The Unity module launched aboard Shuttle Endeavour, the first US piece of the ISS, 1998.
December 5: Shuttle Endeavour launched on STS-108, delivering the Raffaello cargo module to the ISS, 2001.
December 6: The first US satellite launch attempt failed when the Vanguard rocket exploded on the pad, 1957.
December 7: Apollo 17, the last crewed mission to the Moon, launched at night, 1972.
December 8: SpaceX's Dragon capsule flew its first test flight and was recovered from orbit, 2010.
December 9: Shuttle Discovery launched on STS-116, the first night shuttle launch in three years, 2006.
December 10: ESA launched the XMM-Newton X-ray observatory, 1999.
December 11: Apollo 17 astronauts landed in the Taurus-Littrow valley on the Moon, 1972.
December 12: NASA launched Uhuru, the first satellite dedicated to X-ray astronomy, from Kenya, 1970.
December 13: NASA launched the Pioneer 8 probe to study the solar wind, 1967.
December 14: Astronomer Tycho Brahe, whose data underpinned Kepler's laws, was born, 1546.
December 15: Venera 7 became the first spacecraft to soft-land on another planet, Venus, 1970.
December 16: Pioneer 6 launched and went on to outlive its planned mission by decades, 1965.
December 17: Comet Leonard made its closest approach to Venus during its journey through the inner solar system, 2021.
December 18: The SCORE satellite relayed the first voice broadcast from space, a Christmas message, 1958.
December 19: ESA launched the Gaia observatory to map a billion stars in the Milky Way, 2013.
December 20: Shuttle Discovery launched on STS-103 to service the Hubble Space Telescope, 1999.
December 21: Apollo 8 launched, becoming the first crewed spacecraft to orbit the Moon, 1968.
December 22: SpaceX landed a Falcon 9 first stage, the first landing of an orbital rocket booster, 2015.
December 23: Giovanni Cassini discovered Rhea, one of Saturn's largest moons, 1672.
December 24: The crew of Apollo 8 photographed Earthrise and read from Genesis in lunar orbit, 1968.
December 25: The James Webb Space Telescope launched from French Guiana, 2021.
December 26: Simon Marius, who independently discovered and named Jupiter's largest moons, died, 1624.
December 27: Astronomer Johannes Kepler, who formulated the laws of planetary motion, was born, 1571.
December 28: Galileo Galilei observed Neptune through his telescope but mistook it for a fixed star, 1612.
December 29: China launched the uncrewed Shenzhou 4, its final test before a crewed flight, 2002.
December 30: Edwin Hubble announced that Andromeda was a separate galaxy beyond the Milky Way, 1924.
December 31: NASA's GRAIL-A spacecraft entered lunar orbit on New Year's Eve, 2011.
